notes.name: Addie
notes.message:
“Bret, I love you. You make every day of my life better, and I feel beyond lucky to have you as my best friend.”

It’s been ten years since I said those words to you in our vows, and without exception, they have been true every day since. It’s not that every day has been perfect (as you know), but the laughter, contemplation, and productive challenge you bring make my days feel full of love and meaning.

I often find myself reflecting on our life and wondering how I won this lottery. Ten years ago, I had high hopes for what the future would hold, but never could I have imagined this. Two sweet, fun-loving kids full of life and personality; a beautiful home in San Francisco with everything we want and need; an active lifestyle full of adventure, delicious food, and new experiences; and, most importantly, it’s all been created through a strong partnership with the most incredible husband.

Bret, you are the smartest person I know. You grow more handsome by the day. You make me cry with your hilarious humor. Your thoughtful questions inspire me daily. And that’s only half of it. You are an extraordinary father. I deeply admire your never-ending patience and energy for our children. Not to mention, your ability to instantly transform into the silliest and most playful kid on the playground. Truly, you are superhuman, and I couldn’t feel luckier to have you on my team.

As I said ten years ago, I promise to be your lifelong partner, and to work every day to be worthy of your commitment. I can’t wait to see what the next decade with you holds.

Happy Birthday and Happy Anniversary

I love you,

Addie

photos.alt: Addie and Bret at City Hall

notes.name: Bill
notes.message: My favorite of the many many many I have was mostly on the weekends when he was 7,8,9,10ish I was working very hard during the week so often had to use weekends to get all my chores done around the house and out in the yard. While Craig and Joey were watching cartoons Bret used to follow me around while I was working and every time I finished a job he would look at me and say “ What are we going to do next Dad?” He was just a joy to raise.

notes.name: Jill
notes.message: Bret has provided our entire family with many special moments. His athletic and academic achievements from childhood to adulthood are more than any other Wallace or Scattini has ever accomplished. What makes me most proud is watching him truly enjoying being a dad; it gives me peace of mind knowing that Dylan and Penny will be the best that they can be and carry on his legacy of goodness and kindness.

notes.name: Joe
notes.message: One of my favorite memories of Bret was when he essentially forced me up half dome. I was very ready to turn back at the base, but he sat there with me until he was able to muster up the courage in me to get up there.

notes.name: Craig
notes.message: Really a lot to choose from here! Going all the way back to childhood trips to Pine Mountain, exciting hotel stays in San Rafael for baseball tournaments, searching for golf balls in Hawaii, many good times have been had. And even though the technical amount of time spent together in New York City was less than the 2 years we lived in the same apartment, that was a fun part of life and I'm grateful that we were able to do that. Whether it was sharing some of my 5-5-5 deal with Bret as he got home (very) late from work or the occasional times out in the city, it was cool that we got to be together there. And Bret was an integral part of our Rock Band domination!

notes.name: Laurie-Anne
notes.message:
Happy 40th birthday, Bret!

You are truly one of the best people I know. Your intellect, intentionality, and work ethic are unmatched, but what I admire most is the way you move through the world with such deep curiosity and care. You are always armed with thoughtful questions, a sharp perspective, and a genuine desire to understand and support the people around you.

I’m so grateful to have you in my life, as a friend, as family, and as someone whose opinion I deeply value. Wishing you the happiest 40th birthday and a year ahead that reflects back even a fraction of the goodness you bring to everyone around you.

notes.name: Jenny
notes.message:
This past Christmas, I was invited to spend the holiday in Hawaii with Bret and his family.

On Christmas Eve, Dylan and Penny were excitedly tracking Santa with Bret, and we all went to bed a little later than usual.

The next morning, as I stepped out of my room, Dylan came running to show me messages from Santa written on the mirror. We found a stack of written clues for each of us.

The children found their presents… and so did I.

The joy of following the clues to find my presents filled my heart with love.

Bret is truly an amazing person. I feel very grateful and blessed to have him in our family.

Happy 40th birthday! Bret, watching the way you love and care for your family has been one of life’s greatest gifts to me. Thank you for the warmth, joy, and kindness you bring into our lives—you are deeply appreciated and truly loved.

notes.name: A.J.
notes.message:
I’m an old man now and my memory is failing me (Bret will understand once he hits 40), but we’ve made way too many great memories to count them all. Starting with Thanksgiving at One Western Avenue, there have been so many, but a few stand out:

Me feeling like Lance Armstrong while trailing Bret (actual Lance Armstrong) for a solid 100 miles of the 103-mile Tour de Cure. I’ll keep that Strava ride forever.

Trekking the W in Patagonia...from Bret’s tiny chicken to the passport fiasco to the palmitos sandwiches, plus some of the most unreal views we’ve ever seen.

Eating ice cream for the first time ever in Australia, and seeing incredible wildlife on the Great Barrier Reef after an insane boat ride. Barf.

Speaking of barf...the night at Soda Pops, the Wheel of Fortune, and the asterisk on Bret’s lifelong record of never being irresponsible at a bar. Haha!

And then just a huge number of nights with great conversations, games, and time together. Not too flashy alone, but together what make an incredible friendship.

HBD Bret!

photos.alt: A.J., Addie, and Bret biking by the Golden Gate Bridge

notes.name: Edu
notes.message: One of my favorite memories is when we went down to Carmel with the Bertones and while five of us were hanging out, Bret was entertaining all the children by picking them up/having them hang off his different limbs. He seemed to be having as much fun as them. I truly admire how he engages with kids and is fully present and creates a playful environment for all the little ones

notes.name: Kim
notes.message:
When I think of Bret, I always picture him a towering beacon of fun, surrounded by clamoring kids, most of whom are not related to him. Whenever we get together at a park or BADM gathering, the parents socialize with each other while Bret gamely hangs with the kids. He seems to truly enjoy it and the kids do too. Despite his height, he really gets on the kids’ level and creates delightful silliness.

One of my favorite memories was hanging out and having a surprisingly uninterrupted conversation by the pool in Big Island during AJ’s 40th bday week. We were the two non Mauna Kea families and we snuck in some solid QT just talking by the pool, and then a day later, at the Mauna Lani beach.

notes.name: Leah
notes.message: My favorite memory of Bret is an early impression from when we had our first Sandpiper playground meet up. I have never told him this: Dylan had a very normal 2.5 year old meltdown and I saw Bret pull him aside and say "let's blow out candles to calm down". I was shocked-- I was like, are all other dads like this? Can we have a parent coaching session with Bret? 🤣 He is a uniquely level-headed and calm parent and I remember going home and trying out the candle blowing trick with George (and probably sharing with Camille to pick up some of that calm dad energy)!

notes.name: Camille
notes.message:
My favorite 2 memories with Bret are (among many beautiful memories)

1- when you guys where here having coffee with us at our place and Bret shared his thoughts about work, life, and the balance between the 2. It was trusting, vulnerable, and enriching to hear and connect on that level.

2- on my first week at my new job he sent me a very touching text, he was present for me as a friend in that moment.

notes.name: Neil
notes.message:
- When he happily carried Ash on his shoulders on a hike, in part walking backwards to avoid the wind. Ash did not say thank you.

- When he entertained and wrestled 4 kids on a playground while Addie and I played some tennis

- When he had 20 4 year olds chasing him around in a scavenger hunt across Lafayette Park

notes.name: Cat
notes.message:
This is my first true memory of Bret and realizing this guy is legit. There’s an amazing picture of him giving his jump up and down his all, but I can’t find it

In one message, it conveys how he shows up as a parent, how he shows up for kids, and how he shows up for his community. Not to mention how he remembers the little things.

notes.name: Rocío
notes.message:
I know we’ve joked about how “perfect” Bret is, and honestly, knowing someone as thoughtful, kind, and genuine as Bret Wallace has raised the bar for me in ways I didn’t expect. Every interaction I’ve had with him—big or small—has left me smiling or laughing or wondering. Even the simplest moments, like when he’d come home from work and we’d talk for ten minutes, or now, quick catch-ups that somehow feel just as meaningful.

One thing I don’t say enough is how much he keeps me going. In a quiet, steady way, he’s helped me feel more confident in who I am, just by being exactly who he is.

One moment that stays with me: in Hawaii, the waves were rough and the water looked gray and intimidating but he still made the effort to go out and show me the fish. He dove under while I stayed at the surface, trying to catch my breath. Like always, he was enthusiastic and encouraging, and somehow gave me the confidence to keep going. I wouldn’t have stopped unless he told me to.

That’s who he is. Someone who shows up, lifts people up, and makes even the uncertain feel a little more possible. I’m really lucky to know him, and even luckier to learn from him.

Happy birthday, Bret.

Rocío xx

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not true about Bret’s driving record?
choices: Bret failed his first driver’s test
Bret got his license revoked for speeding

questions.prompt: Which of the following thrill-seeking activities has Bret not done?

choices: Bret has a secret love of dessert wine
Bret has once consumed 7+ alcoholic drinks in a single evening
Bret takes a shot of apple cider vinegar after a night of drinking to help with detox

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not true about Bret’s drinking habits?
choices: Bret drinks celery juice every morning to wake up his digestive system
Bret has never had a cup of coffee
Bret enjoys a sweet protein shake every night with dinner

questions.prompt: Which of the following was not a nickname Bret was called as kid?

choices: Running out of an office building

questions.prompt: Bret is a glutton for punishment. Which of the following is not one of his favorite forms of exercise?

Which of the following is not one of Bret’s celebrity crushes?
